tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1.–1.6.) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information). Nastepnie znajdz w tekscie wyrazy lub wyrazenia, ktore odpowiadaja definicjom podanym w punktach 1.7.–1.11. Wymagana jest calkowita poprawnosc ortograficzna odpowiedzi.
tasks.text:
HOW I FOUND MY WAY INTO DOCUMENTARY FILMMAKING

Growing up in a small town in Wales, I never imagined that one day I would be travelling the world with a camera. My father was an electrician and my mother worked as a school nurse, so there was nothing in my upbringing that pointed me towards the arts. Yet from an early age, I was drawn to stories — not books so much as the stories people told in person, with all their gestures and silences.

At university, I studied journalism, which I thought would be the perfect way to combine my love of stories with the practical work of reporting. But I quickly became frustrated with the constraints of newspaper writing, where everything had to be condensed into a few hundred words. I wanted room to breathe, to let a story unfold at its own pace.

The turning point came during my second year, when a lecturer lent me a camera and suggested I document the lives of the fishermen who lived along the coast near our campus. I spent three months following them at sea, learning how they worked, and when I edited the footage together, something clicked. I realised that documentary filmmaking was what I had been looking for all along.

After graduating, I started at the very bottom. I carried equipment, fetched coffee, and did whatever was needed to stay close to the industry. The pay was poor and I was barely able to make ends meet in those early years. I shared a flat with four other aspiring filmmakers and we pooled our resources to buy second-hand editing software. Many of my colleagues gave up and moved on to more stable careers, but I was determined to stick it out.

My first commission came when I was twenty-eight — a short documentary about a community theatre group in Birmingham. The budget was minuscule, so we had to be incredibly resourceful. We shot everything on a shoestring, borrowing equipment and calling in every favour we could. When the film aired on regional television, the response exceeded all our expectations. Critics praised the warmth of the storytelling, and suddenly doors that had previously been shut began to open.

Now in my late forties, I look back on those difficult early years with gratitude. The struggles gave me resilience, and the resourcefulness I developed then still shapes the way I approach every new project. I never take a commission for granted, and I always try to remember what it felt like to be that young person with a borrowed camera, completely in love with the act of telling the truth.

items.statement: The narrator comes from a family with a background in the creative arts.
items.explanation: FALSE — The text states that 'there was nothing in my upbringing that pointed me towards the arts': the narrator's father was an electrician and mother a school nurse.

items.statement: The narrator found newspaper journalism too limiting as a form of storytelling.
items.explanation: TRUE — The narrator 'quickly became frustrated with the constraints of newspaper writing, where everything had to be condensed into a few hundred words'.

items.statement: The fishermen whom the narrator filmed were paid for their participation.
items.explanation: NO INFORMATION — The text describes the filming process but says nothing about whether the fishermen received any payment.

items.statement: The narrator's first professional commission was well received by critics.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text states that 'Critics praised the warmth of the storytelling' after the film aired on regional television.

items.statement: All four of the narrator's flatmates eventually abandoned their filmmaking ambitions.
items.explanation: NO INFORMATION — The text says 'many of my colleagues gave up' but does not specify what the flatmates did with their careers.

items.statement: The narrator believes the hardships of the early career had a lasting positive influence.
items.explanation: TRUE — The narrator says 'The struggles gave me resilience, and the resourcefulness I developed then still shapes the way I approach every new project'.

vocabItems.definition: a moment when an important change takes place
to have just enough money to cover basic living costs
very small in size or amount
spending or using very little money
to continue doing something difficult despite obstacles and problems
